The Wind Circle XXVII 273
If the sky be bereft of the rays of the Sun, the Moon and the
stars, and if there be no rain, then, I think, the month cannot be
called Bhzdrapada.
[The verse in the Anustubh metre is Anar§a or spurious
according to the commentator.]

groups of celestials, the earth filled with the youth of water would
be rich in food crops, and kings would control their rivals and pro-
tect all classes of people with the utmost justice.
